name: Seeking Alpha
description_short: Crowdsourced investment research platform with 16,000+ contributors, Quant Ratings, earnings analysis, and news for stocks, ETFs, and REITs.
description_long:
Seeking Alpha is the largest crowdsourced investment research platform, featuring analysis from over 16,000 contributors alongside professional editorial content and proprietary quantitative ratings. The platform covers stocks, ETFs, REITs, and other investment vehicles with a focus on fundamental analysis and long-term investing.

The platform's unique value lies in its combination of crowdsourced analysis (investment theses from individual contributors vetted by editors), proprietary Quant Ratings (algorithmic ratings based on valuation, growth, profitability, momentum, and EPS revisions), and Wall Street analyst consensus. This three-pronged approach gives investors multiple perspectives on any stock.

Seeking Alpha's free tier provides basic news, some analysis articles, and stock quotes. The Premium plan ($29.99/mo or $239/yr) unlocks all analysis, Quant Ratings, dividend grades, Author Performance tracking, and an ad-free experience. The Pro plan ($499.99/mo) adds exclusive ideas, Top Ideas from highest-performing authors, advanced screening, and a dedicated newsletter. Seeking Alpha has become essential reading for many retail investors, particularly those focused on dividends, value investing, and deep fundamental analysis.
pros: Massive library of crowdsourced analysis and ideas
Quant Ratings provide data-driven stock evaluations
Author Performance tracking shows contributor accuracy
Strong community discussion on every article
cons: Article quality varies widely across contributors
Premium required to access most content
Pro plan is very expensive at $499.99/month
Can create information overload with conflicting analyses
Not suitable for short-term or technical traders
